examples.text: يُسْقَوْنَ مِن رَّحِيقٍ مَّخْتُومٍ ۝ خِتٰمُهُ مِسْكٌ
examples.english: They will be given to drink of full-flavoured sealed wine ۝ and its seal is of musk.

etymology.text: Related to Hebrew רֶקַח (reḥāq, “spiced; spiced wine”), and Akkadian 𒋆𒈨𒌍 (/⁠riqqu, rīqu⁠/, “aromatic plants, beer malt”). Wine, at the advent of the Quranic usage, was served warm and brewed from a dehydrated paste or powder form, with spices and practices similar to modern Arabic coffee culture.
